address: 414 Moo 13, Nang Lae, Muang Chiang Rai

description.en:
## Overview

The Black House, officially known as Baan Dam Museum, is one of the most unconventional art attractions in Thailand. Spread across a vast 160,000-square-meter compound about 10 kilometers north of Chiang Rai city center, the complex consists of more than 40 individual buildings in styles ranging from traditional northern Thai to darkly modern. Despite the nickname "Black Temple" used by some tourists, this is not a religious site but a private art museum and the lifework of one of Thailand's most provocative artists.

## History

Baan Dam was the creation of Thawan Duchanee, a nationally celebrated Thai artist known for his bold exploration of themes like death, sexuality, and the primal forces of nature. Thawan spent decades designing and building the compound, filling it with his paintings, sculptures, and a vast collection of animal bones, skins, horns, and other organic materials. He passed away in 2014, but the museum is maintained by his family and remains exactly as he envisioned it. The dark wood and black color palette that dominate the buildings serve as a deliberate counterpoint to the gleaming White Temple created by his former student, Chalermchai Kositpipat.

## What to See

The main hall is a grand Lanna-style teak building with a soaring black roof, housing carved wooden thrones, crocodile skins, buffalo horns, and striking mixed-media artworks. Surrounding it are dozens of smaller pavilions, each with a distinct architectural personality, from a pointy modernist tower to low-slung traditional structures. One building displays a huge dining table crafted from a single tree trunk, surrounded by carved chairs with horn and bone details. The outdoor areas feature sculptural installations scattered among tropical gardens. The overall atmosphere is eerie, thought-provoking, and unlike anything else in Thailand.

## Practical Info

The museum is open daily from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM with a lunch break from 12:00 to 1:00 PM. Admission is 80 THB per person. There is no dress code since it is a museum rather than a temple. Plan 45 to 60 minutes for a thorough visit. Wear comfortable shoes as the compound is large and mostly outdoors with limited shade. Bring a hat and sunscreen, or an umbrella if visiting during the rainy season from June to October. The Black House pairs well with a visit to the White Temple and Blue Temple for a full day of Chiang Rai's art highlights.
